commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: The component lacks hover states or subtle interactions (like scale effects) for the individual forecast day items.
issues.fix: Wrap forecast items in a clickable or hoverable wrapper to provide better feedback.

issues.description: The forecast section could benefit from more refined spacing or background subtle tinting to distinguish it from the current weather section.
issues.fix: Add a subtle background color using a neutral token for the forecast strip section.

issues.description: The icons used for weather conditions are missing aria-labels to explain the condition to screen readers.
issues.fix: Add aria-label attributes to the icon elements or wrap them in descriptive text containers.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, follows design token usage correctly, and implements all requested features. It is clean and readable, though adding minor interactive flair and better descriptive accessibility for icons would elevate it to production-ready status.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid hierarchy and data handling, but relies on hardcoded color values as fallbacks instead of pure design tokens, and lacks interactive polish like hover states on forecast items. The layout is responsive but could benefit from better mobile considerations for the horizontal forecast strip.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

issues.description: The logic for the review step is currently contained within Step 4, causing the 'Comments' field to appear on the same screen as the review summary.
issues.fix: Separate the review summary into a distinct 5th step or display it conditionally only after the user reaches the final stage.

issues.description: The current structure does not use semantic sectioning or fieldsets for the radio/checkbox groups, and focus management during step changes is not explicitly handled.
issues.fix: Wrap form groups in fieldsets and ensure that navigation between steps moves focus to the new section heading.

issues.description: Lack of visual transition or animation when switching between steps makes the form feel slightly jarring.
issues.fix: Add a simple transition wrapper or frame animation when updating the step state.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and functional, correctly utilizing design tokens and managing state effectively. It meets most requirements, though separating the final review from the comments input would improve the user experience, and better semantic nesting would enhance accessibility.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid data handling and reasonable hierarchy, but significantly fails on design tokens by using hardcoded pixel values (24, 32, 16, 8) instead of var(--ggui-spacing-*) tokens throughout. The layout is mostly correct with proper flex usage, though the step counter could be better integrated into the progress bar visual. Polish is adequate but lacks transition effects and could benefit from more detailed loading/disabled state feedback.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

issues.description: The requirement for 'Due date' display on cards is missing in the implementation.
issues.fix: Add a text element inside the task card component to display task.dueDate.

issues.description: Lack of visual feedback or focus trapping when editing task titles.
issues.fix: Add auto-focus to the input when entering edit mode and implement 'Escape' key handler to cancel editing.

issues.description: The 'Move to' select dropdown within every card consumes too much vertical space, cluttering the UI.
issues.fix: Consider using an icon-based menu or only showing the move controls on hover/expansion.

evaluations.critique: This is a solid, functional Kanban implementation that adheres well to the provided design system. The code is clean and reactive. To reach production quality, the developer should include the missing due-date field and improve the ergonomics of the editing and moving task interactions.

evaluation.critique: The component has a solid Kanban layout with proper flex-based columns and good responsive structure, but uses some hardcoded fallback values in spacing tokens (e.g., '24px', '16px') instead of relying purely on design tokens. Polish is hampered by missing hover/focus states on interactive elements and lack of visual feedback during task transitions. Data rendering is generally correct but the due date field from the Task interface is never displayed, and edit mode lacks confirmation UI clarity.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: Atomic number is centered rather than aligned to the top-left corner of the cell as requested.
issues.fix: Use a flex container with justifyContent: flex-start and alignItems: flex-start inside the cell.

issues.description: No visual feedback on hover for the elements in the grid.
issues.fix: Add a pseudo-class or hover effect to the cell box to change brightness or opacity.

issues.description: Selected element card does not have an aria-live region, so screen reader users aren't notified when selection changes.
issues.fix: Wrap the detail panel in a container with role="region" and aria-live="polite".

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and maps effectively to the provided data props. It correctly implements the complex grid layout and filters. Adding hover states and better focus management would elevate it to a production-ready standard.

evaluation.critique: The component correctly implements a 18-column CSS grid layout with proper positioning and responsive spacing using design tokens. However, hardcoded fallback values (e.g., '#e0f2fe', '#f4f4f5', '4px') appear throughout instead of relying solely on design tokens, and interactive polish is minimal—missing hover/focus states, active indicators for selected elements, and keyboard navigation feedback beyond basic tabIndex.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

example.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

issues.description: The component adds an event listener for 'ggui:agent-data' which was not requested in the prompt.
issues.fix: Remove the custom event listener logic as it introduces unnecessary side-effect management not required by the requirements.

issues.description: State is initialized with props, but there is no useEffect to sync if the parent updates the messages prop later.
issues.fix: Add a useEffect to update chatMessages state when props.messages changes, or remove local state and derive it directly from props.

issues.description: The message grouping logic is correct, but the visual spacing between non-grouped messages and consecutive grouped messages could be more distinct.
issues.fix: Adjust the gap/margin logic to visually group messages closer together than messages from different users.

evaluations.critique: The component is highly functional and meets almost all requirements, including the auto-scroll feature and correct message alignment. The code is clean and uses the design system appropriately. Minor improvements to prop synchronization and removing extraneous event listeners would make it production-ready.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid structure with proper flex layout and scrolling, but uses some hardcoded pixel values (16, 12, 8) instead of design tokens for spacing. The design tokens are mostly correct (using var() for colors), but inconsistent padding usage undermines the token system; also hardcodes 'white' color instead of using a token.

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

issues.description: The border color does not reflect the positive/negative status as requested in the prompt.
issues.fix: Apply the conditional status color to the Card component's border property instead of just the text.

issues.description: The responsive grid template column 'repeat(auto-fit, minmax(300px, 1fr))' does not strictly enforce 3 per row on desktop as requested, potentially wrapping to 2 or 1 depending on container width.
issues.fix: Use a standard responsive grid approach (e.g., gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat(3, 1fr)' for large screens and '1fr' for mobile) to strictly follow the prompt requirements.

evaluations.critique: The component is highly functional and well-structured, adhering strictly to the design system. It handles state updates effectively and provides a clean, readable layout. Minor adjustments to grid constraints and adding status-aware borders would align it perfectly with the original requirements.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid responsive grid layout and good data presentation with proper formatting, but has inconsistent design token usage (hardcoded '16px' padding in Card component, inline style gap values instead of spacing tokens) and lacks interactive polish like hover states or focus indicators on cards. The hierarchy is clear with proper heading levels and whitespace.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.description: The summary view in step 3 maps the raw key strings (e.g., 'emailNotifications') directly to the UI, which is not user-friendly.
issues.fix: Create a mapping object to transform field keys into human-readable labels (e.g., 'emailNotifications' -> 'Email Notifications').

issues.description: Missing aria-live regions or descriptive announcements for step changes.
issues.fix: Add an 'aria-live="polite"' region that updates when the 'step' state changes to inform screen reader users of the progress.

issues.description: Step 3 does not provide a way to 'Edit' specific fields directly, requiring the user to click 'Go Back' multiple times.
issues.fix: Consider adding small edit links/buttons next to summary items to jump to specific steps.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, uses the design system appropriately, and handles form state robustly. The transition logic and validation are solid; improvement in the summary readability and accessibility announcements would make this production-perfect.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid structure and hierarchy, but inconsistent design token usage (hardcoded padding/gaps, missing semantic spacing variables) and poor data presentation on step 3 (raw keys like 'emailNotifications' instead of human-readable labels). Animation reference lacks proper ggui integration.
